figures.caption: Figure 1: An example of a target-guided proactive dialogue system, where the red highlights denote snippets relevant to the current dialogue, which we analyze in detail in App. H.
Table 3: Ablation study on the DuRecDial dataset.

Table 2: Results compared with LLMs. Models labeled ♢ denote fine-tuning, while models labeled ♡ denote the prompt-based method. Bold text indicates the best performance among fine-tuning methods, and underlined text indicates the best performance among prompt-based methods. Significant improvements are marked with † (t-test, p < 0.01) and ‡ (t-test, p < 0.05).
Table 4: Statistics of the DuRecDial and DuRecDial2.0 datasets. Here, "Dial." denotes "dialogue," "Utter." denotes "utterance," and "Trans." denotes "keyword transition."
Table 7: Full experiments on the DuRecDial2.0 dataset with different m values. Bold text highlights the best results, while underlined text indicates the second-best.
Table 5: Full experiments on the DuRecDial dataset with different m values. Bold text highlights the best results, while underlined text indicates the second-best.
Table 6: Full experiments on the DuRecDial dataset with different δ values (when m = 4). Bold text highlights the best results, while underlined text indicates the second-best.
Figure 4: Human evaluation. "App." denotes "appropriateness," "Inf." denotes "informativeness," "Pro." denotes "proactivity," and "Coh." denotes "coherence."
Figure 8: Case study (OOD test set). (a) Results of T5-Flan; (b) Results of our framework; (c) Dialogue-level intent keyword transitions across turns.
Table 14: Visualization of user profile modeling Fu(H u) in the Case Study (ID). Bold highlights the information identified as relevant by the user profile modeling.
Figure 5: Case study (ID test set). (a) T5-Flan; (b) Our framework; (c) Intent keyword transitions.

Figure 7: Human evaluation on the DuRecDial dataset (our framework vs. T5-Zh). "App." denotes "appropriateness," "Inf." denotes "informativeness," "Pro." denotes "proactivity," and "Coh." denotes "coherence."

Table 1: Results compared across advanced models. Bold text indicates the best performance, and underlined text indicates the second-best performance. Significant improvements are marked with † (t-test, p < 0.01) and ‡ (t-test, p < 0.05). The advanced models labeled ⋄ denote the results from our reproduction.
Table 8: Full experiments on the DuRecDial2.0 dataset with different δ values (when m = 3). Bold text highlights the best results, while underlined text indicates the second-best.
Table 9: Ablation study on the DuRecDial2.0 dataset.
